channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Litter picker needed, 8th March
item.description:
Full of random rubbish, this street is never swept or seen a litter picker Please assist

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Peckham Park Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road dipping, 8th March
item.description:
I do not know when the dip first appeared but it is definitely getting worse

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whitehall Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road markings, 8th March
item.description:
The road markings have faded so car drivers along Blackbrook Lane often do not know that they they need to keep this space clear. This space has to be kept clear for the safety of drivers exiting the Elms Estate and Reynard Close.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackbrook Lane, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Peckham park road, 8th March
item.description:
Extremely untidy, full of litter, never seen one litter picker in ages!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Peckham Park Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Significant litter and waste left outside Beths Grammar School entrance on residential street., 8th March
item.description:
A large amount of litter, including takeaway food containers, packaging, bottles and general rubbish, has been left around the entrance gates and litter bins outside Beths Grammar School. The waste is spread across the pavement and roadway rather than contained within the bins. This rubbish has been left in place throughout the entire weekend without being cleared. The bins at this location appear to be insufficient for the volume of waste being generated, resulting in rubbish being discarded around them. This is a residential street and the current condition is unacceptable for local residents, pedestrians and the surrounding area. It creates an unsightly environment and raises concerns around hygiene and vermin if waste continues to accumulate. Photos have been provided showing the scale of the littering. I would ask that the area is cleaned promptly and that the council reviews waste management and bin provision at this location to prevent this ongoing issue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hartford Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Numerous potholes across a small area of road, 8th March
item.description:
Road surface deterioration between number 9 Birdbrook Road and number 18 Birdbrook Road. These potholes are across the same area of road. This issue has come to my attention since the higher volume of traffic has been using the road due to the gasworks on well hall road having permanent closures or temporary traffic lights. The largest pothole size 1000mm x 800mm x 50mm deep. The other (2) potholes in close proximity are 300mm x 300mm x 50mm deep. These have been present for atleast 2 weeks. Rapidly increasing in size.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Birdbrook Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass, 8th March
item.description:
Broken glass on pavement at pedestrian crossing (park side).
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): West Street, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing illegal Parking, 8th March
item.description:
Constant illegal parking across pavements and on double yellow lines, especially on Sundays from late morning onwards.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hertford Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No tax or MOT, 8th March
item.description:
This vehicle has been dumped in this street for over 3 weeks with a smashed window and has not moved We believe it is owned by somebody who lives in Berwick Crescent who has recently been arrested and now in prison so do not see it being moved any time soon.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 65, Berwick Crescent, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another pothole appearing on our roads, 8th March
item.description:
Another pothole appearing on our roads.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Halstow Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bag dumped by Friday, 8th March
item.description:
57-59 Cheval Place. Bag is collecting dog poo and letter

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57-59, Cheval Place, Chelsea, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipping maitress and bed structure, 8th March
item.description:
Someone left 3 mattresses and a bed structure in the communal area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ledbury Street, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Man threw cigarette out of window then parked illegally next to zebra crossing, 8th March
item.description:
The man threw a lit cigarette at pedestrian then parked illegally

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Aldermans Hill, Southgate

item.title: Clamped vehicle left for several weeks, 8th March
item.description:
This car was clamped several weeks ago and has not been moved since. Taking up a residents permit bay.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dumont Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contant rubbish thrown everywhere, 8th March
item.description:
This is a permanent issue

item.title: Constant fly tipping in this spot, 8th March
item.description:
Three large Hackney wheelie bins filled with rubbish the contents of someone’s freezer and personal letters with names and addresses visible.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victorian Grove, Hackney, London

item.title: Constant fly tipping in this area, 8th March

item.title: Vacuum Cleaner, 8th March
item.description:
This vacuum cleaner was dumped at the property late on Saturday 7th March.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Manor Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Bin bags and furniture dumped, 8th March
item.description:
At least 2 matresses, 12 binbags, plastic matting, wood and household waste dumped at the side of Ray Lamb Way. believe it has been added to since I last walked here a week ago.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ray Lamb Way, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
